quoteLast: when Lord Hastings, having taken his bearings and found he was in the designated spot, gave the command to come to the surface.
title: The Boy Allies Under the Sea; Or, The Vanishing Submarines
author: Hayes, Clair W. (Clair Wallace), 1887-

title: The Project Gutenberg Complete Works of Gilbert Parker
author: Parker, Gilbert, 1862-1932

quoteFirst: On the evening of the 31st December the members of the club muster in force at their snug quarters in Twenty-fourth Street. At two or
quoteLast: all the lights are put out, and "Auld lang syne" is sung in chorus, to bid good-bye to the year that is passing away. As soon as the clock has struck the midnight hour[5], the lights are relit, all the company strike up "He's a jolly good fellow," and there is a general hand-shaking and wishing of good wishes for the coming year.
title: Jonathan and His Continent: Rambles Through American Society
author: O'Rell, Max, 1848-1903; Allyn, Jack; Blouët, Mary Bartlett [Translator]

quoteFirst: At about 9 a.m. they began carrying the fuel--wood and kerosene--that was to be used for the cremation, and at
quoteLast: one Londoño, by order of the criminal Macedo, set fire to the unfortunate victims of the Ocainas tribe. This smouldering pile of human flesh remained there until about 10 a.m. on the next day.
title:
The Putumayo, the Devil's Paradise
Travels in the Peruvian Amazon Region and an Account of the Atrocities Committed upon the Indians Therein
author: Hardenburg, W. E. (Walter Ernest), 1886-1942; Casement, Roger, 1864-1916 [Contributor]; Enock, C. Reginald (Charles Reginald), 1868-1970 [Editor]

quoteLast: I had an intermediate ticket: it was quite as good as first-class. The captain sent a sailor to look after me; he dressed, fed, and attended to me in every way he possibly could.
title:

quoteFirst: Ah Wu’s opium den has been already described--except that we saw it before at night, when the place was crowded.  On this occasion there was only one man asleep upon a couch in the lower room.  It was
quoteTime: about twelve o’clock in the morning
quoteLast: , and at this hour, as a general rule, Chinese opium dens are empty, the smokers of the previous evening having departed and the day’s customers not having arrived.
title: Held by Chinese Brigands
author: Gilson, Charles, 1878-1943; De Walton, John [Illustrator]
